arts.ttl: McGinn: We’ve made Villa Park tough place to come
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Newcastle United - Sat, Apr 19th 2025, 17:30 John McGinn is hoping Villa Park proves a fortress for he and his team-mates once again as Aston Villa prepare to take on Newcastle United on Saturday evening. The Villans welcome the Magpies to B6 looking to further close the gap on the Champions League spots of the Premier League table, while aiming to extend their 18-match unbeaten run on home soil in all competitions. McGinn was spurred on by the incredible support from the stands in Tuesday’s Champions League quarter-final second leg tie against PSG on Tuesday, and believes the home supporters can again play a big part in this weekend's "six-pointer". He said: “It gives us massive confidence. We’re really strong at home. We’ve made it a tough place to come, especially over the past few seasons. “But Newcastle are playing really well. They’re strong, they’re a tough team to play against. “I think our record against them at Villa Park over the past few seasons is strong, and they hammered us at St James’ Park earlier this season. “We know how important the game is. They’re in probably their best run of the season since winning the cup, so it’s a huge game for both teams. “They’re obviously five points ahead of us but it’s our job to try and close that gap and stay on their toes. “Definitely, Saturday is a massive game, a six-pointer really.” Reflecting further now the dust has settled on Villa’s Champions League exit, the skipper reiterated his pride in how the side conducted themselves during a first campaign in Europe’s elite competition for 42 years. The Scotland international felt the Paris outfit came up against the full force of Villa Park and how difficult a place it can be to visit, and hopes the positives can drive Unai Emery’s side forward further for the remainder of the season. The 30-year-old added: “Looking back over the campaign, we can be delighted. “We felt we had more, we felt we could have progressed to the semi-final, but over the season, we put in a really good performance. “I think, for us, it was to put ourselves back on the map in European football, and compete with the best teams and best players, and I think we managed to do that. “I think PSG were a little bit shocked and surprised. After they went 2-0 up, I think they thought they were going to have an easy night. “But credit to the lads. We dug deep, and we proved that Villa Park is a really tough place to come.”

arts.ttl: Newcastle United's 'amazing' stay option at St James' Park as 63,000 capacity mooted
arts.sum: Chronicle Live were also first to report in January that the two options would see capacities of around 60,000 to stay at St James' Park and 70,000 for a new stadium. Newcastle United chiefs are confident that both options for their stadium project will have massive appeal to fans once the club is in a position to announce them.

arts.ttl: Atletico Madrid among clubs circling for Aston Villa transfer
arts.sum: Earlier this year, when asked on DAZN if he would like to stay in Spain, Barrenechea said: "Well, without a doubt, although my transfer belongs to Aston Villa. Aston Villa midfielder Enzo Barrenechea has attracted interest from La Liga clubs ahead of the summer transfer window

arts.ttl: Dean Huijsen quizzed over Newcastle United transfer interest as £50m race hots up
arts.sum: This week, Huijsen himself was quizzed over his immediate future and those aforementioned, interested parties as Bournemouth stutter in their European hunt. "I don't read anything, I don't think about it too much, I'm just focusing on my football and working hard."

Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Newcastle United - Sat, Apr 19th 2025, 17:30 Aston Villa look to maintain their push for a top five Premier League finish on Saturday evening, when Newcastle United visit Villa Park. Unai Emery's side are aiming for a fifth consecutive Premier League victory while securing a third straight win in all competitions, having triumphed 3-2 in their Champions League quarter-final second leg meeting with Paris Saint-Germain on Tuesday evening after a 3-0 win at Southampton last weekend. The Villans enter the clash in seventh place, but just three points behind fourth-placed Nottingham Forest in the table, and have won their last five outings at Villa Park in all competitions. The Magpies enter the encounter in a rich vein of form of their own, having won all six of their games since being knocked out of the FA Cup by Brighton & Hove Albion, and already have a trophy to their name this season, lifting the League Cup after a 2-1 win over Liverpool at Wembley. Newcastle will again be under the temporary tutelage of Jason Tindall, with Eddie Howe in hospital recovering from pneumonia. In Tindall's two games in the dugout, his side have scored nine goals and conceded just one, beating Manchester United last Sunday (4-1) and Crystal Palace in midweek (5-0) at St. James' Park. Stats Per 90 minutes in the Premier League this season, Marcus Rashford is having more touches (54), shots (3.8), creating more chances (2.8) and attempting more dribbles (5) for Aston Villa than he did for Manchester United (42 touches, 1.5 shots, 1.4 chances created and 3.5 dribbles). Jacob Murphy has had a hand in 13 goals in his last 17 away Premier League appearances for Newcastle United (4 goals, 9 assists), while he has three assists in seven games against Aston Villa. Aston Villa had 48 touches in Southampton’s box in their 3-0 win last time out, their joint-most in a Premier League game this season. They’re averaging 27.2 touches in the opposition box this season, only having more in one season on record (since 2008/09), which was 28.4 last season. In all competitions, Newcastle United have won eight of their last 10 away matches (L2), with their only two defeats in that time coming at the Etihad and Anfield. Their away win percentage is 53% (10 wins in 19 games), only having a higher percentage in 2016/17 (54%, 14 wins in 26) in their entire history. Aston Villa have missed three of the five penalties they’ve taken in the Premier League this season, a conversion rate of 40%. Among teams to take 5+ pens in a season, this is the lowest by a side since Southampton in 2019/20 (also 2/5, 40%). Since keeping five consecutive clean sheets at Villa Park in March and April 2023, Aston Villa have kept just six clean sheets in their last 37 on home soil. They are unbeaten in 15 home games (W8 D7) despite keeping just two clean sheets in that run. Only Liverpool (35) and Arsenal (28) have won more away Premier League points this season than Newcastle United (27). They’ve won eight away games this season, last winning more in 2001/02 (9). Aston Villa have won each of their last four Premier League matches, conceding just one goal. Villa are looking to win five in a row for the first time since April 2023. Newcastle have won their last three Premier League games against Aston Villa, scoring at least three goals each time. Only against Tottenham have they won more consecutively while scoring 3+ each time in the competition (5 between 2006 and 2008). Aston Villa have lost five of their last six Premier League games against Newcastle (W1), as many as they had in their previous 16 against the Magpies in the division (W4 D7). The Last Meeting The last meeting between these two sides was one to forget as far as Villa were concerned, as Unai Emery's side fell to a 3-0 defeat at St. James' Park on Boxing Day in the reverse fixture. The game was less than two minutes old when Anthony Gordon bent a sublime effort into the far corner to put the hosts ahead, before Villa's task was made more difficult just after the half-hour, when Jhon Durán was shown a straight-red card. Alexander Isak doubled the Toon's advantage just before the hour mark from close range, before Joelinton struck in stoppage time to tie up the three points. Team News Unai Emery will have the pleasure of a fully-fit squad to pick from, having come through Tuesday's Champions League outing unscathed. Leon Bailey was back on the bench against PSG, so is available should he be called upon, while Ross Barkley returned from injury against the Parisians to make his first appearance in over three months. Like the Villans, Newcastle came through their midweek clash - a 5-0 win over Crystal Palace at St. James' Park on Wednesday - without any damage. Their only injury absentees remain Sven Botman, Jamaal Lascelles (both knee) and Lewis Hall (foot), with the former closing in on a return. Harvey Barnes' impressive form could see him keep his place in the starting line-up over Anthony Gordon. Managers' Pre-Match Thoughts “They are now really performing very well, they are winning a lot of matches in a row, getting a lot of points. They are now in their best moment. They have five points more than us. It means a lot, how we are, how they are, how important is the match.” - Unai Emery “It’s going to be an incredibly tough game. We know that. They’ve only lost once at home this season so we know what type of challenge we’ll be facing. They’re trying to achieve the same goal we are. It’ll be two teams going out there to try and win the game.” - Jason Tindall

arts.ttl: Meet Eddie Howe's left-hand man Newcastle United star Lewis Hall loves as close friend steps up
arts.sum: But he is a vital part of the set-up at Newcastle United these days, even if former England coach Jones and Tindall get a little bit more of the limelight. Newcastle United head coach Eddie Howe was admitted to hospital last week after leading the Magpies to a 3-0 win over Leicester City before the club announced he was recovering after being diagnosed with pneumonia.
